---
title: "CU/composite escapement estimates for package-first SPSR intake"
published_date: 2026-03-17
updated_date: 2026-03-19
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
---

Use this recipe when you have **CU-level** or **CU-composite** escapement estimates and need the shortest correct path into SPSR.

```{=html}
<div class="status-badge-row">
  <span class="status-badge status-active">Cookbook</span>
  <span class="status-badge status-canonical">Package-first</span>
  <span class="status-badge status-guidance">Escapement</span>
</div>
```

## Quick summary

- Start with a **salmon data package**, not a standalone upload spreadsheet.
- The canonical package layout is **`metadata/` + `data/`**, with optional root `datapackage.json`.
- The same package-first pattern supports **CU/composite**, **SMU**, and **Population** routes.
- Bulk uploads should use the same package-first source, not a separate branch of ad hoc spreadsheets.
- If you need estimate classification context, use the [Salmon Escapement Estimates Toolkit](https://dfo-pac-sci-dev.shinyapps.io/smn-escapement-toolkit/).

## 1) Confirm the route scope

Choose the SPSR route scope that matches the grain of the table you are packaging:

- **CU/composite** — one row per CU-year or CU-composite-year estimate
- **SMU** — one row per SMU-year estimate
- **Population** — one row per population-year estimate

This recipe is written for **CU/composite** data, but the same package structure carries forward when the data grain changes.

## 2) Assemble the package first

Use the canonical layout:

```text
my-salmon-data-package/
├── datapackage.json          # optional
├── metadata/
│   ├── dataset.csv
│   ├── tables.csv
│   ├── column_dictionary.csv
│   └── codes.csv             # optional
└── data/
    └── cu_composite_escapement.csv
```

Keep the package as the source of truth for both validation and SPSR submission.

## 3) Capture the minimum semantics and provenance

At minimum, document:

- the CU or composite identifier
- the year field used by the dataset
- the escapement value and unit
- estimate type or method context when relevant
- provenance notes for transformations, rollups, or composite rules

Use [GC DFO Salmon Ontology documentation](../reference_info/ontology/formal-documentation.qmd) for canonical IRIs, and record them in `metadata/column_dictionary.csv`.

## 4) Run the one-command pre-flight check

From the repo root, run the package validator before you generate any SPSR upload files.

```bash
Rscript scripts/validate_spsr_package.R path/to/your/salmon-data-package
```

Use `--require-iris` once the measurement semantics are finalized and you want the check to fail on missing IRIs.

```bash
Rscript scripts/validate_spsr_package.R path/to/your/salmon-data-package --require-iris
```

If you prefer to stay in R, the helper is a thin wrapper around the new end-to-end validator:

```r
metasalmon::validate_salmon_datapackage(
  "path/to/your/salmon-data-package",
  require_iris = FALSE
)
```

Treat this as the SPSR pre-flight gate. It catches package/data mismatches, coded-value drift, and dictionary/semantic problems before intake does.

## 5) Prepare the SPSR upload from the package

- download the current templates: <https://spsr.dfo-mpo.gc.ca/download_sdp_templates>
- choose the **CU/composite** route profile that matches the data grain
- generate or align the route-scoped upload CSV(s) from the same package
- keep upload files versioned with the package metadata and data
- if submitting in bulk, use the same package-first route

If you later need to provide **SMU** or **Population** rollups, keep the package-first pattern and switch the route/profile to match the new table grain.

## 6) Submit and archive the accepted version

1. Upload through the SPSR wizard or approved bulk route.
2. Resolve any intake feedback by correcting the package and regenerating the upload file.
3. Record the accepted version, intake date, and route notes.

## Related pages

- [Salmon data package + SPSR intake path](../reference_info/data_standards/ontology-data-package-gpt.qmd)
- [FSAR Data Standardization Workflow](../how_to_guides/fsar-data-standardization-workflow.qmd)
- [FSAR to SPSR: End-to-End Example](../how_to_guides/fsar-to-spsr-end-to-end-example.qmd)
- [Salmon data package specification](../reference_info/data_standards/salmon-data-exchange-package.qmd)
